Tests that multiplying positive infinity by zero results in NaN.

# Purpose
This code is a single assertion test that checks the behavior of multiplying `Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY` by `0` in JavaScript. The test uses the `assert` function to verify that the result of this operation is `NaN` (Not-a-Number). This is a narrow functionality test that ensures the JavaScript engine correctly handles this specific arithmetic operation according to the IEEE 754 standard for floating-point arithmetic. The assertion will pass if the expression evaluates to `true`, confirming the expected behavior.
